name: Lock In Duolingo authenticated XP
description: Proves control of a Duolingo profile through a self-only authenticated endpoint, then proves the same stable profile id, username and cumulative XP without changing the player's profile.

securityModel.accountOwnership: A self-only privacy-settings request returns 200 for the authenticated profile, 403 for another profile in the same session, and 401 without the session. Only the constant setting id disable_social is revealed; setting values are not revealed.
securityModel.profileBinding: Both required requests use the same duolingo_user_id template. The profile response id must equal that requested id and the server-resolved public username id.
securityModel.baseline: A fresh snapshot is accepted atomically with create/join and becomes the minimum consumed XP.
securityModel.completion: Only new totalXp above both the lock baseline and the globally consumed value can count.
securityModel.antiReplay: The identity and totalXp snapshot form a global nullifier; one XP range cannot settle multiple days or locks.
securityModel.verificationFriction: The player signs in to Duolingo inside the isolated Reclaim flow if needed. No Duolingo profile field is edited.
securityModel.knownLimit: This proves control of a profile and XP credited by Duolingo, not human learning or the absence of account sharing or automation. Duolingo is not an official partner.

requestData.purpose: Prove the active session controls the requested stable profile id
Prove the same profile's stable id, username and cumulative XP

runtimePolicy.metricMode: monotonic delta after fresh atomic baseline
